zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Rice於1612年11月18日出生在英國素福爾可斯坦斯亞德時，她的父親Henry Rice29歲，母親Elizabeth Frost24歲。她於1633年11月17日與John Joseph Moore Jr.在英國賀特福爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1690年12月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利享年78歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Rice于1612年11月18日出生在英国素福爾可斯坦斯亞德时，她的父亲Henry Rice29岁，母亲Elizabeth Frost24岁。她于1633年11月17日与John Joseph Moore Jr.在英国賀特福爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1690年12月14日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利享年78岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利。

nameMeanings.name: Rice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized pronunciation of one of the most common Welsh personal names, Rhys, from a form originally meaning ‘rash, impetuous’, also spelled Rys and Re(e)s. See also Reese , with which it is interchangeable as a result of different Anglicized forms of the Welsh vowel y, and also compare Preece and Price . Initial R- in Welsh is voiceless and often spelled Rh-, but in English R- is voiced as in the Anglicized surnames Rees and Rice. Welsh y is a short back vowel /ɪ/. In the medieval period the English approximation of this vowel was either /i/ or /e/, lengthened to /i:/ and /e:/. Subsequent sound changes in English produced the alternative pronunciations represented in Rees, Preece and Rice, Price. The name has also been established in Ireland from an early date.

English: either a topographic name for someone who lived in or near a thicket (Middle English ris, rice, ris, from Old English hrīs, Old Norse hrís), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place called with this word, such as Rise (East Yorkshire).

English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English Rys(e) and Re(e)s which when without a preposition could derive from one or other of several Old French and Middle English words, including Anglo-Norman French ris ‘laughter, smile’, Middle English ris, res ‘stem, stalk’, in origin the same word as in 2 above, and Middle English ris, rise, rice, res, Old French ris, riz ‘rice’, perhaps a nickname for a rice dealer or a cook.

variants.name: Reese
meanings: North German and Dutch (De Reese): nickname for a very big man, from Middle Low German, Middle Dutch rese ‘giant’.
German: habitational name from any of the places called Rees or Reese, in Rhineland and Lower Saxony.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: German: habitational name from a place in the Rhineland called Rees.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Andreas  .

variants.name: Ryce
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of   Rice  .

variants.name: Rise
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Aase, Lief, Sven.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of over twenty farmsteads named Rise, from Old Norse hrís ‘brushwood’. The name also occurs in Sweden and Denmark.

variants.name: Wrice
meanings: Probably Welsh or English: variant of   Rice  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Rice was born on 18 November 1612, in Stanstead, Suffolk, England, her father, Henry Rice, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Frost, was 24. She married John Joseph Moore Jr. on 17 November 1633, in Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 14 December 1690, in Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 78, and was buried in Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Rice est née le 18 novembre 1612, à Stanstead, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Henry Rice, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Frost, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé John Joseph Moore Jr. le 17 novembre 1633, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 14 décembre 1690, à Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterrée à Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Rice è nata il il 18 novembre 1612 nel Stanstead, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Henry Rice, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Frost, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato John Joseph Moore Jr. il 17 novembre 1633, nel Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 14 dicembre 1690, nel Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolta a Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Riceが1612年11月18日にイギリススフォルクスタンステアドで生まれた時，父Henry Riceは29歳，母Elizabeth Frostは24歳であった。1633年11月17日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアでJohn Joseph Moore Jr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1690年12月14日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックススドブリーで78歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックススドブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Rice(이)가 1612년 11월 18일 영국 수폴크 스탠스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Rice(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Frost(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1633년 11월 17일 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 John Joseph Moore Jr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1690년 12월 14일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Rice nasceu em 18 de novembro de 1612, em Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Rice, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Frost, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com John Joseph Moore Jr. em 17 de novembro de 1633, em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 14 de dezembro de 1690, em Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 78 anos, e foi sepultada em Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Rice родилась 18 ноября 1612 г. в местности станстид, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry Rice было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Frost, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Joseph Moore Jr. 17 ноября 1633 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 14 декабря 1690 г. в местности судбури, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 78 лет и была похоронена в местности судбури, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Rice nació el 18 de noviembre de 1612, en Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Rice, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Frost, tenía 24 años. Se casó con John Joseph Moore Jr. el 17 de noviembre de 1633, en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 14 de diciembre de 1690, en Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultada en Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
